April 2019 Australian coal producer Atrum Coal confirmed deposit premium hard coking coal Elan South mine project Alberta, Canada.
Testing coal shows that coke strength after reaction (CSR) 71pc, which similar premium hard coking coal produced Canadian mining firm Teck Resources with 72pc.
"Elan South hard coking coal expected comparable highly competitive with, premium hard coking coal currently exported from Canada Australia," company said.
Other specifications coking coal from Elan South include fluidity dial divisions minute (ddpm), crucible swelling number (CSN) 7.5, (air dried), volatile matter 25.9pc, sulphur level 0.65pc 0.05pc phosphorous.
Testing coal carried three laboratories CoalTech Germany Incar Spain.
Atrum last month raised A$20mn ($14.2mn) from stock sale Australian Stock Exchange help fund development Elan hard coking coal mine.

Robin Chapman author fourteen collections poetry, including Parallel Press chapbook Only Everglades World Posner Poetry Award-winning books (Tebot Bach) Images Complex World: Poetry Chaos (with J.C. Sprott's fractals explanations; World Scientific).
book, Dreamer Counted Dead, received Wisconsin Library Association Outstanding Poetry Book Year Award, book Abundance received Cider Press Editors' Book Award.
collaborated with composers, painters, textile artists scientists producing work, including traveling Epidemic Peace Imagery show poets artists.
posts watercolors, other poets' poems, Robin Chapman's Poem Blog.
held writing residencies Banff Centre Arts Leighton Studios Vermont Studio Center recipient 2010 Appalachia Poetry Prize, Wisconsin Arts Board Literary Arts Fellowship.
Fellow Wisconsin Academy Sciences, Arts, Letters life member WFOP.
teaches week-long poetry workshops Clearing Bjorklunden.
Robin Chapman's poems have appeared many print online journals, including Alaska Quarterly Review, American Scholar, Appalachia, Christian Science Monitor, Hudson Review, Poetry, Valparaiso Poetry Review Poetry Daily, Verse Daily, American Life Poetry, Writers' Almanac.
eelgrass meadow (Tebot Bach 2011), Dappled Things (Paris: Revue 2013), portfolio poems accompanying Peter Miller's photogravures, Hundred White Pelicans (Tebot Bach, 2013) most recent books; True Things will from Tebot Bach 2016.
also co-edited anthologies Retirement (with Judith Strasser, University Iowa Press) Love Over Anthology Women's Poems (with Jeri McCormick, Mayapple Press).

universe spun nothing?
